evals.prompt: I'm building an Android app using Kotlin Compose Multiplatform. I want to add a frosted glass / glassmorphism effect to my bottom navigation bar. The nav bar should float at the bottom center as a pill/capsule shape, with blur behind it so content scrolls underneath. I need dark mode and light mode support. Please generate all necessary Kotlin files with complete working code.
evals.expected_output: Complete Kotlin implementation with liquid library dependency, LiquidState management, liquefiable/liquid modifiers, dark/light theme parameter tables, glass indicator drawBehind code, and platform compatibility fallback

assertions.text: Output includes liquid library dependency (io.github.fletchmckee.liquid)
Output includes rememberLiquidState() or LiquidState creation
Output includes liquefiable modifier usage
Output includes .liquid() modifier with frost parameter
Output includes dark/light theme parameter variation
Output includes CircleShape for capsule navigation bar
Output includes platform compatibility fallback (expect/actual or API level check)
Output includes CompositionLocal for LiquidState distribution

evals.prompt: I have a Compose Multiplatform app and I want to create a glass-style tab bar at the top of my screen, similar to Apple's liquid glass effect. It should show categories like 'Trending', 'Popular', 'New' and have a glowing pill indicator that slides between them. The whole bar should be translucent with blur. How do I implement this?
evals.expected_output: Complete LiquidGlassCategoryChips composable with liquid modifier, animated pill indicator using drawBehind, glass color definitions, and dark/light theme support

assertions.text: Output includes liquid modifier with glass parameters (frost, refraction, etc.)
Output includes drawBehind for glass indicator
Output includes specular highlight or gradient glass effect
Output includes Animated indicator with spring physics
Output includes RoundedCornerShape for filter chips container
Output includes dark/light theme glass color definitions

evals.prompt: 我在做一个KMP项目，想给整个应用添加毛玻璃效果——底部导航栏和顶部标签栏都需要。我需要知道怎么配置这个 liquid 库、怎么管理状态、以及怎么让内容在玻璃面板后面正确地模糊显示。
evals.expected_output: Complete setup guide covering dependency configuration, LiquidState creation and CompositionLocal distribution, liquefiable content registration pattern, and integration of both bottom nav and top filter chips with glass effects

assertions.text: Output includes liquid library dependency in gradle version catalog format
Output explains separate LiquidState per glass panel
Output includes liquefiable modifier for background content
Output includes CompositionLocalProvider pattern
Output covers both bottom nav and top bar integration
Output includes Android API 33+ compatibility check

evals.prompt: I'm working on a KMP app and want to add a glassmorphism effect to a BottomSheet and a Dialog component. The sheet/dialog should blur the content behind it with a frosted glass look. Is this possible with the liquid library? How would I set it up?
evals.expected_output: Explanation of how to apply liquid glass to BottomSheet/Dialog surfaces, with LiquidState setup for modal surfaces, liquefiable content behind the modal, and fallback behavior

assertions.text: Output mentions applying liquid glass to modal/sheet surfaces
Output includes liquefiable for content behind the modal
Output includes frost/blur parameter configuration
Output addresses the modal overlay use case specifically

evals.prompt: I want to use the liquid glass effect on my Compose Multiplatform app, but I need to support Android API 26+ devices. How do I handle devices that don't support the RuntimeShader-based blur? Show me a complete fallback strategy.
evals.expected_output: Complete expect/actual platform check, graceful degradation to opaque background with border, and conditional modifier chain that toggles between glass and fallback

assertions.text: Output includes expect/actual platform compatibility check
Output references Android API level threshold (TIRAMISU / API 33)
Output includes fallback UI (opaque background or border)
Output shows conditional modifier chain (if glass available use liquid, else fallback)
